core_mechanism: a critical-threshold (spiked-matrix / BBP) phase transition in the spectrum of a large random matrix, in which an outlier eigenvalue detaches from the continuous noise bulk and its eigenvector localizes once a signal-to-noise coupling exceeds a critical value, diagnosed jointly by eigenvalue separation from the bulk edge and by an eigenvector participation-ratio (localization vs. delocalization) criterion
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on the Baik–Ben Arous–Péché transition at the Marčenko–Pastur spectral edge, where signal directions separate from noise only above a critical strength, and it retains components by combining that spectral separation with a participation-ratio delocalization test on the eigenvectors.
